header.name: DataFlow To Graph
header.description: Discover available data flows in the application. Returns flow names, descriptions, modules, types, and deployment status. Use dataflow_details to get full version and deployment details for a specific flow.

flowName.description: Filter by flow name (partial match, case-insensitive). Examples: 'DataModel', 'Dynamic', 'Inventory'. Leave empty string for all flows. Returns flows whose name contains this value.

flowTypeId.description: Filter by exact flow type ID. Examples: 'system' (platform flows), 'integration' (external integrations), 'business' (business logic). Leave empty string for all types.

activeOnly.description: When true (default), returns only active/deployed flows. When false, returns all flows including inactive drafts. Use false for development exploration.

first.description: Maximum number of flows to return (1-500). Defaults to 100. Use higher values when exploring the full flow catalog.

flowName.description: Filter by flow name (partial match, case-insensitive). Leave empty for all flows.

flowTypeId.description: Filter by exact flow type ID. Leave empty for all types.

header.name: DataModel To Graph
header.description: Discover available data models in the WMS. Returns model names, descriptions, modules, element counts, and relation fields. Use data_flow_data_model_details to get full element details for a specific model.

modelName.description: Filter by model name (partial match, case-insensitive). Examples: 'Product', 'Inventory', 'Storage'. Leave empty string for all models. Returns models whose name contains this value.

moduleName.description: Filter by exact module name. Examples: 'Inventory Tracking', 'Order Processing', 'Master Data'. Leave empty string for all modules. Must match exactly (case-sensitive).

systemOnly.description: When true, returns only backend/system models (internal platform models). When false, returns all models including business models (Product, Inventory, etc.). Defaults to false.

first.description: Maximum number of models to return (1-1000). Defaults to 100. Use higher values when exploring the full schema.

modelName.description: Filter by model name (partial match, case-insensitive). Leave empty for all models.

moduleName.description: Filter by exact module name. Leave empty for all modules.

systemOnly.description: When true, returns only backend/system models. When false (default), returns all models.

first.description: Maximum number of models to return (1-1000). Default 100.

moduleName.description: Filter by exact module name. Leave empty for all modules.

first.description: Maximum number of models to return (1-1000). Default 100.

header.name: Dynamic Mutate
header.description: Execute create, update, or delete mutations on any data model. Supports dryRun mode to validate mutations without executing them. Use data_flow_data_model_to_graph first to discover available models.

modelName.description: The PascalCase name of the model to mutate (e.g., 'Product', 'InventoryStatus', 'StorageUnit'). Use data_flow_data_model_to_graph to discover available models and their fields.

operation.description: The mutation type: 'create' inserts a new record, 'update' modifies an existing record by id, 'delete' removes a record by id.

data.description: Field values as JSON object or JSON string. For 'create': provide all required fields (use data_flow_data_model_details to see field requirements). For 'update': provide only fields to change. For 'delete': not required (can be empty {} or omitted).

id.description: The record ID to update or delete. REQUIRED for 'update' and 'delete' operations - will error if missing. Not used for 'create' (id is auto-generated). Use data_flow_dynamic_query to find record IDs.

returnFields.description: Fields to return after mutation. Defaults to ['id', 'code', 'name', 'active'] based on what exists on the model. For 'delete', always returns only ['id']. Invalid fields are filtered with warnings in response. Use data_flow_data_model_details to see available fields.

dryRun.description: If true, validates mutation without executing. Returns: generated GraphQL mutation, variables, validated fields, and any warnings. Use to preview and verify mutations before execution. Defaults to false.

modelName.description: The PascalCase name of the model to mutate (e.g., 'Product', 'InventoryStatus', 'StorageUnit'). Use data_flow_data_model_to_graph to discover available models and their fields.

operation.description: The mutation type: 'create' inserts a new record, 'update' modifies an existing record by id, 'delete' removes a record by id.

data.description: Field values as JSON object or string. For 'create': provide all required fields (use data_flow_data_model_details to see requirements). For 'update': only fields to change. For 'delete': not required (can be {} or omitted).

id.description: The record ID to update or delete. REQUIRED for 'update' and 'delete' - will error if missing. Not used for 'create' (id is auto-generated). Use data_flow_dynamic_query to find record IDs.

returnFields.description: Fields to return after mutation. Defaults to ['id', 'code', 'name', 'active'] based on what exists on model. For 'delete', always returns only ['id']. Invalid fields are filtered with warnings. Use data_flow_data_model_details to see available fields.

dryRun.description: If true, validates mutation without executing. Returns: generated GraphQL mutation, variables, validated fields, and warnings. Use to preview and verify mutations before execution.

data.responseTransform:
{
  "error": true,
  "errorType": "EXECUTION_ERROR",
  "errorMessage": $state.lastError.message ? $state.lastError.message : "Mutation execution failed",
  "errorDetails": $state.lastError,
  "failedAt": $._result ? "5. Format Response" : $._mutation ? "4. Execute Mutation" : $._fields ? "3. Build Mutation" : $._schema ? "2. Validate Operation" : $._input ? "1. Fetch Schema" : "Validate Input",
  "model": $._model ? $._model : $._input.modelName,
  "mutation": $._mutation,
  "variables": $._variables,
  "input": $._input
}

returnFields.description: Fields to return after mutation. Defaults to ['id', 'code', 'name', 'active'] based on what exists on model. For 'delete', always returns only ['id']. Invalid fields are filtered with warnings. Use data_flow_data_model_details to see available fields.

dryRun.description: If true, validates mutation without executing. Returns: generated GraphQL mutation, variables, validated fields, and warnings. Use to preview and verify mutations before execution.

header.name: Dynamic Query
header.description: Execute dynamic read queries against any data model. Supports filtering, field selection, related model includes, sorting, and pagination. Use data_flow_data_model_to_graph first to discover available models and their fields.

modelName.description: The PascalCase name of the model to query (e.g., 'Product', 'StorageUnit', 'InventoryStatus', 'StorageZone', 'Inventory'). Use data_flow_data_model_to_graph to discover available models and their fields.

where.description: Filter conditions as JSON string using GraphQL WhereInput syntax. Operators: _eq (equals), _contains (substring match), _in (array of values), _gt/_gte/_lt/_lte (comparisons). Examples: {"code":{"_eq":"ABC"}}, {"name":{"_contains":"Orange"}}, {"code":{"_in":["A","B","C"]}}, {"active":{"_eq":true}}. Use empty string or {} for no filter.

select.description: Fields to return. Defaults to ['id', 'code', 'name', 'description', 'active'] if empty. Complex types (Measure fields like height, weight, length, width) auto-expand to {value, unit{id,code}}. Use dot notation for specific subfields: 'height.value' returns only value, 'height.unit' returns unit with id/code. Invalid fields are silently filtered out.

include.description: Related models to include (relation fields from schema). Examples: ['storageZone'] for StorageUnit, ['product', 'storageUnit', 'inventoryStatus'] for Inventory. Each included relation returns {id, code}. Invalid relations are silently filtered out.

orderBy.description: Sort order as JSON string with field name and direction ('asc' or 'desc'). Examples: {"name":"asc"}, {"code":"desc"}. Only one field supported. Use empty string or {} for default order.

first.description: Maximum records to return (1-1000). Defaults to 100 if not specified.

after.description: Pagination cursor from previous response's endCursor. Use empty string for first page. Response includes hasNextPage, hasPreviousPage, and endCursor for navigation.

modelName.description: The PascalCase name of the model to query (e.g., 'Product', 'StorageUnit', 'InventoryStatus', 'StorageZone', 'Inventory'). Use data_flow_data_model_to_graph to discover available models and their fields.

where.description: Filter conditions using GraphQL WhereInput syntax (object or JSON string). Operators: _eq (equals), _contains (substring), _in (array), _gt/_gte/_lt/_lte (comparisons). Examples: {"code":{"_eq":"ABC"}}, {"name":{"_contains":"Orange"}}, {"active":{"_eq":true}}. Use {} for no filter.

select.description: Fields to return. Defaults to ['id', 'code', 'name', 'description', 'active'] if empty. Complex types (Measure fields like height, weight, length, width) auto-expand to {value, unit{id,code}}. Use dot notation for specific subfields: 'height.value' returns only value, 'height.unit' returns unit with id/code. Invalid fields are silently filtered.

include.description: Related models to include (relation fields from schema). Examples: ['storageZone'] for StorageUnit, ['product', 'storageUnit', 'inventoryStatus'] for Inventory. Each included relation returns {id, code}. Invalid relations are silently filtered.

orderBy.description: Sort order as object or JSON string: {fieldName: 'asc'|'desc'}. Examples: {"name":"asc"}, {"code":"desc"}. Only one field supported. Use {} for default order.

first.description: Maximum records to return (1-1000). Defaults to 100 if not specified.

after.description: Pagination cursor from previous response's endCursor. Use null or omit for first page. Response includes hasNextPage, hasPreviousPage, and endCursor for navigation.

data.transform: $merge([$, { "_result": $query({ "statement": $._query, "variables": $._variables }) }])

data.responseTransform:
{
  "error": true,
  "errorType": "EXECUTION_ERROR",
  "errorMessage": $state.lastError.message ? $state.lastError.message : "Query execution failed in Try/Catch block",
  "errorDetails": $state.lastError,
  "failedAt": $._model ? "Execute Query or Format Response" : $._schema ? "Build Fields or Build Query" : $._input ? "Fetch Schema" : "Validate or earlier",
  "model": $._model,
  "query": $._query,
  "variables": $._variables,
  "input": $._input
}

Examples: {"code":{"_eq":"ABC"}}, {"name":{"_contains":"Orange"}}, {"active":{"_eq":true}}. Use {} for no filter.

Use dot notation for specific subfields: 'height.value' returns only value, 'height.unit' returns unit with id/code. Invalid fields are silently filtered.

include.description: Related models to include (relation fields from schema). Examples: ['storageZone'] for StorageUnit, ['product', 'storageUnit', 'inventoryStatus'] for Inventory. Each included relation returns {id, code}. Invalid relations are silently filtered.

orderBy.description: Sort order as object or JSON string: {fieldName: 'asc'|'desc'}. Examples: {"name":"asc"}, {"code":"desc"}. Only one field supported. Use {} for default order.

first.description: Maximum records to return (1-1000). Defaults to 100 if not specified.

after.description: Pagination cursor from previous response's endCursor. Use null or omit for first page. Response includes hasNextPage, hasPreviousPage, and endCursor for navigation.

{
  "error": true,

  "errorMessage": $state.lastError.message ? $state.lastError.message : "Query execution failed in Try/Catch block",

header.name: Screen Details
header.description: Get detailed information for a specific screen including elements, routes, versions, deployments, and substitution relationships. Use screen_to_graph first to discover available screen IDs.

screenId.description: The exact ID of the screen to get details for (e.g., 'inventoryTable', 'productsTable', 'orderLineCreateWidget'). Use data_flow_screen_to_graph first to discover available screen IDs. Returns elements, routes, versions, deployments, and substitution relationships.
The exact ID of the screen to get details for. Use screen_to_graph first to discover available screen IDs.
The exact ID of the screen to get details for. Use screen_to_graph first to discover available screen IDs.

header.name: Screen To Graph
header.description: Discover available screens in the application. Returns screen names, descriptions, modules, routes, and deployment status. Use screen_details to get full element details for a specific screen.

screenName.description: Filter by screen name (partial match, case-insensitive). Examples: 'Products', 'Inventory', 'Order'. Leave empty string for all screens. Returns screens whose name contains this value.

moduleName.description: Filter by exact module name. Examples: 'Inventory Tracking', 'Order Processing', 'Master Data'. Leave empty string for all modules. Must match exactly (case-sensitive).

activeOnly.description: When true (default), returns only active/deployed screens. When false, returns all screens including inactive drafts. Use false for development exploration.

includeWidgets.description: When true, includes widget screens (reusable UI components). When false (default), excludes widgets and returns only full page screens.

first.description: Maximum number of screens to return (1-500). Defaults to 100. Use higher values when exploring the full UI structure.

screenName.description: Filter by screen name (partial match, case-insensitive). Leave empty for all screens.

moduleName.description: Filter by exact module name (e.g., 'Inventory Tracking'). Leave empty for all modules.

activeOnly.description: When true (default), returns only active screens. When false, returns all screens.

includeWidgets.description: When true, includes widget screens. When false (default), excludes widgets.

first.description: Maximum number of screens to return (1-500). Default 100.

screenName.description: Filter by screen name (partial match, case-insensitive). Leave empty for all screens.

moduleName.description: Filter by exact module name (e.g., 'Inventory Tracking'). Leave empty for all modules.

activeOnly.description: When true (default), returns only active screens. When false, returns all screens.

includeWidgets.description: When true, includes widget screens. When false (default), excludes widgets.

first.description: Maximum number of screens to return (1-500). Default 100.

payload.name: Applications

payload.name: Abelare MCP Tools
